calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Dissolution Of Right To Attach Order And Protective Order, And Return Of Undertaking
Off Calendar Jul-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KARL S. KRONENBERGER, Esq. of Kronenberger Rosenfenfeld, LLP (Tel: 415-955-1155) for plaintiff Zea Gallery;
Defendant DIANA ROTHERMEL (Tel: 415-999-1031) in pro per.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ZEA GALLERY's Motion For Entry Of Judgment Dissolution Of Right To Attach Order And Protective Order, And Return Of Undertaking.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's "motion for entry of judgment, dissolution of right to attach order and protective order and return of undertaking" is granted. Summary adjudication of plaintiff's conversion claim was granted on June 3, 2022; it then dismissed the rest of its claims. Rather than address the pending motion's merits, defendant's opposition argues a statute of limitations defense. That argument should have been raised, if at all, in defendant's summary adjudication papers, but was not.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Continued To Aug-01-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Karl S. Kronenberger, Esq., KRONENBERGER ROSENFELD, LLP, (415) 955-1155,karl@KRInternetLaw.com, for Plaintiff Zea Gallery,
Diana Rothermel, 415-999-1031, drothermel@hotmail.com, Defendant in Pro Per.

Defendant seeks clairfication of the Court's tentative ruling.

Matter is not contested/argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff ZEA GALLERY's motion for summary adjudication on its second cause of action for conversion is granted.

Plaintiff maintained its burden of production to establish a claim for conversion based on the Zemach declaration and its attachments. (See CCP 437c(p)(1); Voris v. Lampert (2019) 7 Cal. 5th 1141, 1150 [elements of conversion claim].) In opposition, defendant fails to introduce any controverting evidence to create a triable issue of material fact. Rather, defendant seeks a continuance or denial of the motion per CCP 437c(h). A declaration in support of a request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h) must show: (1) the facts to be obtained are essential to opposing the motion; (2) there is reason to believe such facts may exist; and (3) the reasons why additional time is needed to obtain these facts. (See Cooksey v. Alexakis (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 246, 254.) "It is not sufficient under the statute merely to indicate further discovery or investigation is contemplated. The statute makes it a condition that the party moving for a continuance show 'facts essential to justify opposition may exist.'" (Roth v. Rhodes (1994) 25 Cal. App. 4th 530, 548.) Here, defendant fails to highlight what facts she hopes to unearth that will create a triable issue of material fact. Since defendant's declaration does not comply with CCP 437c(h), the motion is granted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-06-2022 Continued To Jun-03-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant DIANA ROTHERMEL in pro per;
No other appearances made in this case.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ZEA GALLERY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The Court notes that defendant Ms. Rothermel did not file an opposition and did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling.

The Court grants a continuance of the matter to June 3, 2022 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302.

Defendant Ms. Rothermel must file and serve her opposition no later than May 13, 2022.

Plaintiff is to file and serve its reply no later than May 20, 2022.

Court to inform plaintiff's counsel of continuance by email.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/SRB)
